name: Chroma
what_it_does: Open-source AI-native embedding database that runs in-process (embedded) or as a server, designed for local-first RAG development and simple production deployments.
use_cases: Local RAG prototyping and development without external infrastructure
Lightweight agent memory for single-user or small-scale applications
Embedding-based document retrieval with metadata filtering
In-process semantic search in Python scripts and notebooks
Persisted local vector store for offline or edge AI applications
not_for: Large-scale production deployments with millions of vectors (use Pinecone or Qdrant)
Teams needing enterprise SLA and managed cloud reliability
Distributed multi-node deployments at high throughput
best_when: You're prototyping RAG locally or need a zero-infrastructure embedded vector store that just works in Python without setup.
avoid_when: You need production scale, high availability, or enterprise compliance features.

auth.notes: In-process mode has no auth. Server mode supports a static auth token via CHROMA_SERVER_AUTHN_CREDENTIALS. Chroma Cloud uses API key auth. Auth is minimal by design for developer simplicity.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Fully free for self-hosted; Chroma Cloud free tier available
pricing.paid_tiers: Chroma Cloud pricing not yet publicly detailed
pricing.notes: Apache-2.0 open source. Self-hosting is completely free. Chroma Cloud is the managed offering. Most users run it locally or self-hosted.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Python exceptions with descriptive messages. REST errors are simple JSON with error field. Error quality is adequate but not exceptional.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Documents added with explicit IDs are idempotent (upsert behavior). Add without IDs always creates new entries — agents should always specify IDs.
known_agent_gotchas: In-process mode is not thread-safe for concurrent writes — use server mode for multi-threaded agents
Collection names are case-sensitive and have naming restrictions (letters, numbers, hyphens, underscores only)
Metadata values must be strings, integers, floats, or booleans — nested objects are not supported
No built-in embedding model — must provide embeddings or configure an embedding function
Query results include distance (not similarity) by default — lower is better, opposite of some other DBs
Persistence requires specifying a path — default in-memory mode loses all data on restart
